hypothesis: If an XGBoost model is trained via ∆-learning on ST-135, RAC, and APRDF descriptors to predict the difference between GFN1-xTB and DFT (PBE/HSE06) band gaps for MOFs, then its reconstructed DFT band gap predictions will have lower error than direct-learning models and fine-tuned MOFTransformer/CGCNN baselines because ∆-learning captures and corrects systematic biases in the semi-empirical xTB baseline.
